Command-line interface for running agents, managing agencies, and controlling swarms

import { Aside, Tabs, TabItem } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

The animaOS CLI (`animaos`) is your command-and-control center for running agents, managing agencies, and launching swarms — all from the terminal.

## Installation

```bash
bun add -g @animaOS-SWARM/cli
# or use npx without installing
npx @animaOS-SWARM/cli --help
```

<Aside type="note">
  The CLI communicates with the animaOS daemon over HTTP. Make sure the daemon is running before executing commands.
</Aside>

## Commands

### `animaos run`

Run a single agent or a swarm with a one-off task.

```bash
# Single agent
animaos run "Summarize the Rust book" -m gpt-4o-mini

# Swarm with supervisor strategy
animaos run "Build a landing page" -s supervisor -m gpt-4o
```

| Flag | Default | Description |
|------|---------|-------------|
| `-m, --model` | `gpt-4o-mini` | Model to use |
| `-p, --provider` | `openai` | Provider: `openai`, `anthropic`, `ollama`, `openrouter` |
| `-n, --name` | `task-agent` | Agent name (single-agent mode) |
| `-s, --strategy` | — | Swarm strategy: `supervisor`, `dynamic`, `round-robin` |
| `--api-key` | — | API key override |
| `--no-tui` | — | Force plain text output |

### `animaos chat`

Start an interactive REPL session with an agent.

```bash
animaos chat -m claude-3-5-sonnet -p anthropic
```

Type your messages at the `you >` prompt. Type `exit` to quit.

### `animaos create`

Create either a single agent workspace or a full agent agency. With no arguments, the CLI asks whether you want an agent or agency. Agency mode uses configured remote provider credentials when available and falls back to a deterministic local scaffolder when no provider key is present.

```bash
# Interactive mode: choose agent or agency
animaos create

# Single-agent workspace
animaos create agent medicine-helper \
  -d "Research medical literature and summarize risks clearly"

# Full agency workspace
animaos create agency content-team \
  -d "Research topics and write high-quality articles" \
  -s 5 \
  --seed

# Legacy shorthand still creates an agency
animaos create content-team --yes
```

| Flag | Default | Description |
|------|---------|-------------|
| `--kind` | — | `agent` or `agency`; equivalent to `animaos create agent ...` |
| `-p, --provider` | auto | Provider for generation/runtime config. Uses configured remote credentials first, otherwise `deterministic` |
| `-m, --model` | `gpt-4o-mini` | Model for generation |
| `--models` | — | Comma-separated model pool for agents |
| `-d, --description` | — | What the agent or agency does |
| `-s, --size` | `4` | Agency team size (2–10) |
| `--system` | — | System prompt for single-agent mode |
| `--seed` | — | Generate starter memories for each agent |
| `-y, --yes` | — | Accept the generated team without confirmation |

**Output structure:**

```
content-team/
├── anima.yaml        # Team config
├── org-chart.mmd     # Mermaid diagram
├── README.md         # Mission, values, roster
└── agents/
    ├── orchestrator/
    │   ├── profile.md
    │   ├── assets/
    │   └── memory/
    │       └── seed.json   # (if --seed)
    └── worker-1/
        ├── profile.md
        ├── assets/
        └── memory/
```

### `animaos launch`

Launch an agency from its `anima.yaml` config.

```bash
cd content-team

# One-shot task
animaos launch "Write an article about Rust macros"

# Interactive mode (REPL)
animaos launch

# With TUI
animaos launch --tui
```

| Flag | Default | Description |
|------|---------|-------------|
| `--api-key` | — | API key override |
| `--no-tui` | — | Disable TUI, use plain text |

**Interactive commands:**

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `exit` | Quit |
| `/help` | Show available commands |
| `/health` | Recheck daemon connectivity |

### `animaos agents`

Inspect daemon-backed agents.

```bash
# List all agents
animaos agents list

# Show details for a specific agent
animaos agents show researcher
```

### `animaos mod`

Enable, disable, or list mods.

```bash
animaos mod enable my-mod
animaos mod disable my-mod
animaos mod list
```

## Environment

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | OpenAI credentials |
| `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` | Anthropic credentials |
| `OLLAMA_HOST` | Ollama server URL |
| `ANIMAOS_WORKSPACE_ROOT` | Workspace root for mod config |

What is animaOS, how it works, and what you can build with it.

import { Card, CardGrid, Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

**animaOS** is a host-agnostic agent runtime designed for building, deploying, and operating autonomous AI agents at scale. It separates the core intelligence engine from host-specific concerns like HTTP, databases, and filesystems — so your agent logic stays pure, testable, and portable across any environment.

At its heart, animaOS is three things:

1. **A runtime engine** that manages agent lifecycles, executes tools, handles LLM provider calls, and maintains state.
2. **A memory system** with hybrid recall — combining BM25 lexical search, vector semantic search, temporal reasoning, and relationship graphs.
3. **A swarm coordinator** that orchestrates multi-agent collaboration with configurable strategies.

Whether you're building a single research assistant or a fleet of cooperating agents, animaOS gives you the primitives to do it without vendor lock-in.

## Philosophy

<CardGrid>
  <Card title="Host-Agnostic Core" icon="seti:rust">
    The Rust core knows nothing about HTTP, databases, or filesystems. It operates on pure data structures and snapshots. Hosts provide the "reality" — the core provides the behavior.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Composable by Design" icon="seti:typescript">
    Agents are configured, not coded. Tools, plugins, providers, and evaluators snap together like LEGO. Reuse components across projects without rewriting boilerplate.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Memory-First" icon="seti:db">
    Agents remember. The memory system isn't an afterthought — it's a first-class citizen with search, recall, evaluation, retention, and relationship tracking built in.
  </Card>
</CardGrid>

## Architecture

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                      Your Application                       │
│              (SDK, CLI, or custom integration)              │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                        Host Layer                           │
│  ┌─────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────┐ │
│  │ Rust Daemon │  │ Elixir Host  │  │  Python Service     │ │
│  │ (ready)     │  │ (planned)    │  │  (planned)          │ │
│  └─────────────┘  └──────────────┘  └─────────────────────┘ │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                      Core Runtime                           │
│  ┌──────────┐  ┌──────────────┐  ┌───────────────────────┐  │
│  │anima-core│  │ anima-memory │  │   anima-swarm         │  │
│  │- agent   │  │ - BM25       │  │   - coordinator       │  │
│  │  runtime │  │ - vectors    │  │   - message bus       │  │
│  │- events  │  │ - temporal   │  │   - strategies        │  │
│  │- tools   │  │ - relations  │  │                       │  │
│  └──────────┘  └──────────────┘  └───────────────────────┘  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

The **core** is where all the intelligence lives. It has zero I/O dependencies — no HTTP framework, no database driver, no filesystem access. This makes it trivial to test, embed in other applications, or port to new environments.

The **host** wraps the core and exposes it over a runtime boundary. The production-ready Rust host provides an Axum HTTP/SSE server. Other hosts (Elixir, Python) are planned for future releases.

<Aside type="note">
  With Postgres-backed daemon persistence enabled, tool steps are written durably and can be reused on an explicit retry. Reuse is request-keyed, not prompt-keyed: callers must repeat the same `retryKey` or `idempotencyKey` in request metadata for a retried run to reuse completed tool results.
</Aside>

## Key Concepts

### Agents

An agent is an autonomous LLM-powered runtime with a configuration, state, and optional tools. You define what it knows, how it behaves, and what it can do — the runtime handles the rest.

- **Configuration** — name, model, provider, system prompt, personality traits
- **Tools** — typed actions the agent can invoke (web search, file I/O, memory access)
- **Plugins** — reusable bundles of tools, providers, and evaluators
- **State** — runtime status, message history, token usage

[Learn more about Agents →](/sdk/agents/)

### Swarms

A swarm is a coordinated group of agents that collaborate on a shared task. The swarm runtime manages turn-taking, message passing, and result synthesis.

- **Strategies** — `round-robin`, `supervisor`, or `dynamic` delegation
- **Manager** — the lead agent that orchestrates the group
- **Workers** — agents that execute subtasks and report back
- **Events** — real-time SSE stream of everything happening inside the swarm

[Learn more about Swarms →](/sdk/swarms/)

### Memories

The memory system gives agents long-term recall through a hybrid retrieval pipeline.

- **Search** — BM25 + vector similarity for finding relevant memories
- **Recall** — fuses lexical, vector, relationship, temporal, recency, and importance signals
- **Evaluation** — automatic deduplication and quality scoring before storage
- **Retention** — configurable decay and cleanup to prevent unbounded growth
- **Relationships** — graph connections between agents, users, and entities

[Learn more about Memories →](/sdk/memories/)

## Getting Started in 60 Seconds

```bash
# 1. Start the daemon
bun run daemon

# 2. Set your provider key
export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."

# 3. Create and run an agent
npx -y @animaOS-SWARM/sdk-cli agent create --name helper --model gpt-4o-mini
```

Or use the TypeScript SDK for full programmatic control:

```typescript
import { agent, createDaemonClient } from '@animaOS-SWARM/sdk';

const client = createDaemonClient();

const helper = await client.agents.create(
  agent({
    name: 'helper',
    provider: 'openai',
    model: 'gpt-4o-mini',
    system: 'You are a helpful assistant.',
  })
);

const result = await client.agents.run(helper.state.id, {
  text: 'What is the capital of France?',
});

console.log(result.result.data?.text);
```
Create, run, and manage agents with the animaOS SDK
Search, recall, and manage agent memories with the animaOS SDK

import { Aside, Tabs, TabItem } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

The memory system in animaOS provides **hybrid recall** combining BM25 lexical search, vector semantic search, temporal reasoning, and relationship graph traversal. Memories can belong to agents, rooms, worlds, or sessions, and support scoped visibility.

## Create a Memory

```typescript
const memory = await client.memories.create({
  agentId: 'agent-uuid-here',
  agentName: 'researcher',
  type: 'fact',
  content: 'Rust daemon memory endpoint created',
  importance: 0.8,
  tags: ['sdk', 'memory'],
  scope: 'shared',
  roomId: 'room-1',
  worldId: 'world-default',
});
```

### `CreateMemoryInput` fields

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `agentId` | `string` | ✅ | Owning agent identifier |
| `agentName` | `string` | ✅ | Agent name at creation time |
| `type` | `MemoryType` | ✅ | Memory category |
| `content` | `string` | ✅ | Memory text |
| `importance` | `number` | ✅ | Importance score (0–1) |
| `tags` | `string[] \| null` | — | Optional searchable tags |
| `scope` | `MemoryScope` | — | Visibility: `shared`, `private`, or `room` |
| `roomId` | `string` | — | Room context |
| `worldId` | `string` | — | World context |
| `sessionId` | `string` | — | Session context |

### `MemoryType` values

Common types include `fact`, `reflection`, `conversation`, `goal`, `plan`, `observation`, and `event`. The type is stored as a string and can be extended for your domain.

### `MemoryScope` values

| Value | Behavior |
|-------|----------|
| `shared` | Visible to all agents in the same context |
| `private` | Visible only to the owning agent |
| `room` | Visible to agents in the specified room |

## Search Memories

Search uses a hybrid of lexical (BM25) and vector similarity scoring.

```typescript
const results = await client.memories.search('daemon memory', {
  agentName: 'researcher',
  type: 'fact',
  limit: 5,
  minImportance: 0.5,
});

for (const result of results) {
  console.log(`[${result.score.toFixed(3)}] ${result.memory.content}`);
}
```

### `MemorySearchOptions`

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `agentId` | `string` | Filter by owning agent |
| `agentName` | `string` | Filter by agent name |
| `type` | `MemoryType` | Filter by memory type |
| `scope` | `MemoryScope` | Filter by visibility scope |
| `roomId` | `string` | Filter by room |
| `worldId` | `string` | Filter by world |
| `sessionId` | `string` | Filter by session |
| `limit` | `number` | Maximum results |
| `minImportance` | `number` | Minimum importance threshold |

### `MemorySearchResult`

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `memory` | `Memory` | The matched memory |
| `score` | `number` | Combined relevance score |

## Recent Memories

Retrieve the most recently created memories with optional filtering:

```typescript
const memories = await client.memories.recent({
  agentId: 'agent-uuid-here',
  scope: 'shared',
  limit: 20,
});
```

### `RecentMemoriesOptions`

Same fields as `MemorySearchOptions` except `type` and `minImportance`.

## Recall

**Recall** is the advanced retrieval API. It fuses multiple signals into a single ranked result set:

- **Lexical** — BM25 keyword matching
- **Vector** — Semantic embedding similarity
- **Relationship** — Graph proximity via `AgentRelationship`
- **Temporal** — Time-decayed relevance
- **Recency** — How recently the memory was created
- **Importance** — The stored importance score

```typescript
const results = await client.memories.recall('evidence probe', {
  entityId: 'user-1',
  agentId: 'agent-1',
  temporalLimit: 7,
  limit: 3,
});

for (const r of results) {
  console.log(`score ${r.score.toFixed(3)} — ${r.memory.content}`);
  console.log(`  lexical: ${r.lexicalScore.toFixed(3)}`);
  console.log(`  vector: ${r.vectorScore.toFixed(3)}`);
  console.log(`  relationship: ${r.relationshipScore.toFixed(3)}`);
}
```

### `MemoryRecallOptions`

All `MemorySearchOptions` plus:

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `entityId` | `string` | Target entity for relationship scoring |
| `recallAgentId` | `string` | Agent performing the recall |
| `lexicalLimit` | `number` | Max lexical candidates |
| `recentLimit` | `number` | Max recent candidates |
| `relationshipLimit` | `number` | Max relationship candidates |
| `temporalLimit` | `number` | Temporal window size |

### `MemoryRecallResult`

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `memory` | `Memory` | The recalled memory |
| `score` | `number` | Fused composite score |
| `lexicalScore` | `number` | BM25 contribution |
| `vectorScore` | `number` | Embedding similarity contribution |
| `relationshipScore` | `number` | Graph proximity contribution |
| `temporalScore` | `number` | Temporal relevance contribution |
| `recencyScore` | `number` | Recency contribution |
| `importanceScore` | `number` | Importance contribution |

## Evaluate and Store

Before storing a memory, you can run it through the evaluation pipeline. The evaluator decides whether to **store**, **merge** with an existing memory, or **ignore** the input.

```typescript
const outcome = await client.memories.addEvaluated({
  agentId: 'agent-1',
  agentName: 'researcher',
  type: 'fact',
  content: 'User prefers concise release notes',
  importance: 0.4,
  tags: ['preference'],
  minContentChars: 8,
  minImportance: 0.1,
});

console.log(outcome.evaluation.decision); // 'store' | 'merge' | 'ignore'
console.log(outcome.evaluation.reason);   // Human-readable explanation
console.log(outcome.evaluation.score);    // Confidence score

if (outcome.memory) {
  console.log('Stored memory ID:', outcome.memory.id);
}
```

### `MemoryEvaluationDecision`

| Value | Meaning |
|-------|---------|
| `store` | Save as a new memory |
| `merge` | Combine with an existing duplicate memory |
| `ignore` | Discard — too low quality or redundant |

### `MemoryEvaluationOutcome`

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `evaluation` | `MemoryEvaluation` | Evaluation result |
| `memory` | `Memory \| null` | The stored/merged memory, if any |

### Evaluate without storing

If you want to preview the evaluation decision before committing:

```typescript
const evaluation = await client.memories.evaluate({
  agentId: 'agent-1',
  agentName: 'researcher',
  type: 'fact',
  content: 'Something happened',
  importance: 0.5,
});

console.log(evaluation.decision, evaluation.reason);
```

## Memory Trace

Trace the evidence graph for a specific memory — its relationships and linked entities.

```typescript
const trace = await client.memories.trace('memory-uuid-here');

console.log('Memory:', trace.memory.content);
console.log('Relationships:', trace.relationships.length);
console.log('Entities:', trace.entities.length);
```

### `MemoryEvidenceTrace`

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `memory` | `Memory` | The traced memory |
| `relationships` | `AgentRelationship[]` | Connected relationships |
| `entities` | `MemoryEntity[]` | Linked entities |

## Retention

Apply automatic memory cleanup with configurable decay and importance thresholds.

```typescript
const report = await client.memories.applyRetention({
  minImportance: 0.2,
  maxMemories: 1000,
  maxAgeMillis: 30 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000, // 30 days
  decayHalfLifeMillis: 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000, // 1 day
});

console.log('Decayed:', report.decayedMemories.length);
console.log('Removed:', report.removedMemoryIds.length);
console.log('Relationships removed:', report.removedRelationshipIds.length);
```

### `MemoryRetentionInput`

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `maxAgeMillis` | `number` | Delete memories older than this |
| `minImportance` | `number` | Delete memories below this importance |
| `maxMemories` | `number` | Keep only the N most important |
| `decayHalfLifeMillis` | `number` | Half-life for exponential importance decay |

### `MemoryRetentionReport`

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `decayedMemories` | `MemoryImportanceAdjustment[]` | Memories whose importance was reduced |
| `removedMemoryIds` | `string[]` | Deleted memory IDs |
| `removedRelationshipIds` | `string[]` | Deleted relationship IDs |

## Readiness

Check whether the memory subsystem is fully operational — embeddings loaded, vector store ready, and evaluation pipeline passing self-checks.

```typescript
const ready = await client.memories.readiness();

console.log('Overall:', ready.passed);
console.log('Vector count:', ready.embeddings.vectorCount);
console.log('Embedding provider:', ready.embeddings.provider);
console.log('Model:', ready.embeddings.model);
console.log('Dimension:', ready.embeddings.dimension);
console.log('Evaluation checks:', ready.evaluation.passedChecks, '/', ready.evaluation.totalChecks);
```

### `MemoryReadiness`

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `passed` | `boolean` | Overall health status |
| `embeddings` | `MemoryEmbeddingStatus` | Embedding engine status |
| `evaluation` | `MemoryEvalReport` | Self-test results |

### `MemoryEmbeddingStatus`

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `enabled` | `boolean` | Whether embeddings are active |
| `provider` | `string` | Embedding provider name |
| `model` | `string` | Model identifier |
| `dimension` | `number` | Vector dimensionality |
| `vectorCount` | `number` | Number of vectors stored |
| `persisted` | `boolean` | Whether vectors are persisted |
| `storageFile` | `string \| null` | Path to vector store file |

## Relationships

Memories are connected via relationships between agents and entities. You can create and query these relationships explicitly.

### Create a relationship

```typescript
const relationship = await client.memories.createRelationship({
  sourceAgentId: 'agent-a',
  sourceKind: 'agent',
  targetAgentId: 'agent-b',
  targetKind: 'agent',
  relationshipType: 'collaborator',
  strength: 0.9,
  confidence: 0.85,
});
```

### Query relationships

```typescript
const relationships = await client.memories.relationships({
  agentId: 'agent-a',
  relationshipType: 'collaborator',
  minStrength: 0.5,
  limit: 10,
});
```

### `AgentRelationshipOptions`

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `agentId` | `string` | Filter by participating agent |
| `entityId` | `string` | Filter by entity |
| `sourceKind` | `RelationshipEndpointKind` | Filter by source type |
| `sourceAgentId` | `string` | Filter by source agent |
| `targetKind` | `RelationshipEndpointKind` | Filter by target type |
| `targetAgentId` | `string` | Filter by target agent |
| `relationshipType` | `string` | Filter by relationship type |
| `roomId` | `string` | Filter by room |
| `worldId` | `string` | Filter by world |
| `sessionId` | `string` | Filter by session |
| `minStrength` | `number` | Minimum relationship strength |
| `minConfidence` | `number` | Minimum confidence |
| `limit` | `number` | Maximum results |

## Entities

Entities are typed objects that can participate in the relationship graph.

```typescript
// Create an entity
const entity = await client.memories.createEntity({
  kind: 'user',
  id: 'user-42',
  name: 'Alice',
  aliases: ['al', 'ali'],
  summary: 'A power user who prefers CLI tools.',
});

// List entities
const entities = await client.memories.entities({
  kind: 'user',
  limit: 50,
});
```

### `MemoryEntity`

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `kind` | `RelationshipEndpointKind` | Entity type |
| `id` | `string` | Unique identifier |
| `name` | `string` | Display name |
| `aliases` | `string[]` | Alternative names |
| `summary` | `string \| null` | Optional description |
| `createdAt` | `number` | Unix timestamp (ms) |
| `updatedAt` | `number` | Unix timestamp (ms) |
Get up and running with the animaOS SDK in minutes
Complete SDK API reference with types, errors, and helper functions

import { Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

The animaOS SDK is organized around a single `DaemonClient` with three domain-specific sub-clients: `AgentsClient`, `SwarmsClient`, and `MemoriesClient`.

The SDK communicates with any animaOS-compatible host over HTTP and SSE. The [Rust daemon](https://github.com/G9000/animaOS-SWARM/tree/main/hosts/rust-daemon) in this repo is the reference implementation. Hosts that implement the same HTTP contract (agents, swarms, memories, health endpoints) work out of the box.

## `DaemonClient`

```typescript
import { createDaemonClient, DaemonClient } from '@animaOS-SWARM/sdk';

const client = createDaemonClient(options);
```

### Constructor options (`DaemonClientOptions`)

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `baseUrl` | `string` | `http://127.0.0.1:8080` | Daemon HTTP endpoint |
| `fetch` | `FetchLike` | `globalThis.fetch` | Custom fetch implementation |

### Methods

| Method | Signature | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|
| `health()` | `() => Promise<DaemonHealth>` | Check daemon status |
| `requestJson<T>(path, init?)` | `(string, RequestInit?) => Promise<T>` | Generic typed JSON request |
| `subscribe<T>(path, init?)` | `(string, RequestInit?) => AsyncGenerator<DaemonEvent<T>>` | SSE event stream |

### `DaemonHealth`

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `status` | `string` | Health status: `ok`, `degraded`, or `unavailable` |

### `DaemonEvent<T>`

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `event` | `string` | SSE event name |
| `data` | `T` | Parsed JSON payload |

---

## `AgentsClient`

Accessed via `client.agents`.

| Method | Signature | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|
| `create` | `(config: AgentConfig) => Promise<AgentSnapshot>` | Create a new agent |
| `list` | `() => Promise<AgentSnapshot[]>` | List all agents |
| `get` | `(agentId: string) => Promise<AgentSnapshot>` | Get agent by ID |
| `run` | `(agentId: string, input: Content) => Promise<AgentRunResponse>` | Execute a task |
| `recentMemories` | `(agentId: string, options?) => Promise<AgentMemory[]>` | Fetch recent memories |

### `AgentSnapshot`

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `state` | `AgentState` | Runtime state |
| `messageCount` | `number` | Total messages processed |
| `eventCount` | `number` | Total events emitted |
| `lastTask` | `TaskResult<Content> \| null` | Result of the most recent task |

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `agent` | `AgentSnapshot` | Updated agent snapshot |
| `result` | `TaskResult<Content>` | Task result |

| `createdAt` | `number` | Unix timestamp |
| `tags` | `string[] \| null` | Tags |

## `SwarmsClient`

Accessed via `client.swarms`.

| Method | Signature | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|
| `create` | `(config: SwarmConfig) => Promise<SwarmState>` | Create a swarm |
| `get` | `(swarmId: string) => Promise<SwarmState>` | Get swarm state |
| `run` | `(swarmId: string, input: Content) => Promise<SwarmRunResponse>` | Execute swarm task |
| `subscribe` | `(swarmId: string, init?) => AsyncGenerator<DaemonEvent<SwarmStreamEventPayload>>` | SSE stream |

### `SwarmRunResponse<T>`

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `swarm` | `SwarmState` | Final swarm state |
| `result` | `TaskResult<T>` | Synthesized result |

### `SwarmState`

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `id` | `string` | Swarm ID |
| `status` | `string` | Runtime status |
| `agentIds` | `string[]` | Participating agents |
| `tokenUsage` | `TokenUsage` | Cumulative tokens |

### `SwarmStreamEventPayload`

A discriminated union of:

| Type | Key fields |
|------|-----------|
| `SwarmEventPayload` | `swarmId`, `state`, `result` |
| `SwarmMessagePayload` | `swarmId`, `message` |
| `SwarmAgentEventPayload` | `agentId`, `agentName` |
| `SwarmTaskFailedPayload` | `agentId`, `agentName`, `error` |
| `SwarmToolBeforePayload` | `agentId`, `agentName`, `toolName`, `args` |
| `SwarmToolAfterPayload` | `agentId`, `agentName`, `toolName`, `status`, `durationMs` |
| `SwarmAgentTokensPayload` | `agentId`, `agentName`, `usage` |

## Helper Functions

These are typed identity functions that provide full IntelliSense when defining configurations.

| Function | Signature | Purpose |
|----------|-----------|---------|
| `agent(config)` | `<T extends AgentConfig>(config: T) => T` | Typed `AgentConfig` helper |
| `swarm(config)` | `<T extends SwarmConfig>(config: T) => T` | Typed `SwarmConfig` helper |
| `action(config)` | `<T extends Action>(config: T) => T` | Typed `Action` helper |
| `plugin(config)` | `<T extends Plugin>(config: T) => T` | Typed `Plugin` helper |

---

## Errors

| Class | Thrown When | Properties |
|-------|-------------|------------|
| `DaemonHttpError` | Daemon returns non-2xx status | `status: number`, `body: unknown` |
| `DaemonConnectionError` | Network failure reaching daemon | `cause: unknown` |

### Example: structured error handling

```typescript
import { DaemonHttpError, DaemonConnectionError } from '@animaOS-SWARM/sdk';

try {
  const result = await client.agents.run(agentId, { text: 'Hello' });
} catch (error) {
  if (error instanceof DaemonHttpError) {
    console.error(`Daemon error ${error.status}:`, error.body);
  } else if (error instanceof DaemonConnectionError) {
    console.error('Cannot reach daemon:', error.cause);
  } else {
    throw error;
  }
}
```
Multi-agent coordination with the animaOS SDK
Terminal UI for visual swarm monitoring and agent interaction

import { Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

The animaOS Terminal UI (TUI) is a visual interface built with [Ink](https://github.com/vadimdemedes/ink) that runs inside your terminal. It gives you a real-time dashboard for watching swarms collaborate, inspecting agent activity, and sending tasks.

## Launch

```bash
# Launch an agency with TUI
cd content-team
animaos launch --tui

# Or force TUI for a one-off run
animaos run "Build a website" -s supervisor --tui
```

## Layout

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  animaOS  │  content-team  │  supervisor   │
├───────────┴────────────────┴───────────────┤
│  ┌─────────┐  ┌─────────────────────────┐  │
│  │ Agents  │  │ Message Stream          │  │
│  │ ★ Alice │  │ Alice > Researching...  │  │
│  │ • Bob   │  │ Bob > Drafting...       │  │
│  │ • Carol │  │                         │  │
│  └─────────┘  └─────────────────────────┘  │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │ Result Log                             │ │
│  │ • Task completed — 12.3s               │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  task > _                                   │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Status: running  │  Tokens: 4,231          │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Panels

| Panel | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| **Agents** | Shows all agents in the swarm with their role (★ orchestrator, • worker) and status |
| **Message Stream** | Real-time messages and events between agents |
| **Tool Panel** | Displays tool invocations, arguments, and results as they happen |
| **Result Log** | History of completed tasks with duration and token usage |
| **Input Bar** | Type tasks and slash commands |
| **Status Bar** | Swarm status, total token usage, and daemon health indicator |

## Slash Commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `/help` | Show available commands |
| `/health` | Check daemon connectivity |
| `/clear` | Clear the message stream |
| `/history` | Toggle history view |

## Keyboard Shortcuts

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| `Enter` | Submit task |
| `Tab` | Cycle between panels |
| `Ctrl+C` | Quit |

## Live Events

The TUI subscribes to the daemon's SSE event stream, so you see everything in real time:

- Agent status changes (`idle` → `running` → `completed`)
- Tool invocations with arguments and results
- Token usage updates per agent
- Swarm completion events

<Aside type="tip">
  The TUI works best in a terminal that supports true color and at least 80×24 characters. Resize your terminal if panels feel cramped.
</Aside>
